engine.ladder_reasoning: Visitor browses the photography itself — the full-bleed grid of project galleries IS the proof; awards and press credits merely reinforce work that stands on its own.

goal.one_action: browse the photo projects and then get in touch (newsletter signup / email for assignments & prints)

structure.archetype_note: Squarespace photographer portfolio: full-bleed image-tile home grid linking into per-project essay+gallery pages, plus about/published/contact; Projects and Education are dropdown parents.

mapped.observed_as: Awards list (Carmignac, World Press Photo, Deloitte Grant)
Published — press/publication credits (TIME, NYT, Guardian, Nat Geo)
Based between Paris and Caracas / mailto / Instagram

confidence.why: fetch_quality=full; clear photographer portfolio archetype; nav and sections captured. Minor: only 5 of 6 allowed non-home pages sampled (Education/Private Mentoring uncrawled), but core intent unambiguous.

gap_findings: The 'Published' page is an outbound list of press/publication credits acting as proof (where her work appeared) — currently expressible only as socialProof/text; a 'press-credits' proof pattern (distinct from logo-wall imagery) is a mild gap.

Education > Private Mentoring is a secondary offering (Calendly single-step embed) grafted onto a portfolio site — a portfolio that also sells a service/mentoring is a dual-intent shape the single-action model does not cleanly capture.
